compiled_state.title: As a developer, validate that nava-platform app install runs fully headlessly
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Spike task to validate that nava-platform app install can run fully non-interactively (no TTY) for all 3 nava-platform templates (NextJS, Flask, Rails).
**Why**: The epic's install path depends on headless CLI operation. copier.yml files define interactive questions that must be pre-seeded via --data flags. If any question prompts interactively, the onboarding flow will hang.
**Scope**:
- IN: Write a CI script that runs nava-platform app install with no TTY for each template. Enumerate all copier.yml questions per template and supply via --data. Test that omitting a required --data flag produces a non-zero exit (not a hang). Verify both uv and pipx install paths.
- OUT: Implementing the actual onboarding integration — this is validation only.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a CI script exists that installs nava-platform via uv, then runs app install for NextJS, Flask, and Rails templates with no TTY, and all exit 0
  ← Satisfies: "Prerequisites: Spike validates headless nava-platform app install"
- When this story is complete, the script also tests that omitting a required --data flag for each template produces a non-zero exit code (not a hang/timeout)
  ← Satisfies: "Prerequisites: Test omitted --data -> non-zero exit"
- When this story is complete, all required --data flag names and default values per template are documented in a findings file
  ← Satisfies: "Registry config file defines required --data flags"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] nava-platform installed from GitHub HEAD — no version pin. Document exact commit tested.
- [Testing] Run with timeout to detect hangs (copier blocking on stdin).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Spike complete — validation script created, all 10 tests GREEN, findings documented.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
